description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a hip roof covered in reddish-brown tiles and beige stucco exterior walls. The structure features decorative cornerstones and a prominent central section on one side, flanked by symmetrically placed windows with metal grilles. A covered porch with a tiled gable roof and supporting columns marks the main entrance, accessible by a short flight of steps. The house includes a wraparound balcony on one side, supported by white columns and protected by railings. The windows are rectangular and regularly spaced, and the overall design is reminiscent of Mediterranean or neoclassical suburban homes.
description: A beige two-story house with a hip tile roof, decorative stonework, a covered porch, and a wraparound balcony.
description_view_0: This view shows the main entrance with a covered porch, tiled gable roof, and supporting columns.
description_view_1: This view highlights the central decorative section, symmetrical windows, and the absence of a porch.
description_view_2: This side view reveals the wraparound balcony with white columns and railings, as well as a single window on the ground floor.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the full length of the balcony, the rear entrance with steps, and additional windows.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with tiled gable roof, supporting columns, and a short flight of steps leading to the main door.
